user_message: Can you extract the deployment blockers from this email thread for Nod Publishers? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Nod Publishers: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: Customer 360 unavailable, Kusto BI fallback needed

required_artifacts: SL4-level forecast rollup with source attribution

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF31-J trap (Fallback BI query joins on mismatched dimensions); produces complete SL4-level forecast rollup with source attribution, hierarchy alignment, period boundary reconciliati
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF31-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF31-J failure mode (Fallback BI query joins on mismatched dimensions)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF31-J -- Fallback BI query joins on mismatched dimensions
